text.tspan: MULTI-TIER STORAGE: hot/warm/cold/archival tiers.
Move data between tiers automatically based on access pattern.
LIFECYCLE / TIERING POLICIES
u2022 'age-based': move objects >30d old to IA, >90d to Glacier
u2022 'access-based': S3 Intelligent-Tiering auto-promotes/demotes by access pattern
u2022 'class-based': different prefixes get different storage classes
u2022 'manual / app-driven': app decides when to tier (e.g., closed ticket u2192 archive)
COMMON ARCHITECTURE
app reads from hot first u2192 falls through to warm u2192 cool u2192 archive
retrievals from archive trigger restoration jobs (minutesu2013hours)
users see 'archived' status; restore-on-demand UX
PITFALLS
u2022 retrieval cost surprises: small frequent reads from cold tiers can cost MORE than just keeping it warm
u2022 latency surprises: 'cold' isn't slower for first byte; it's slower because of restore time
u2022 minimum storage durations: cool/archive often charge for early deletion
u2022 metadata still on hot tier: object listing/search needs warm DB
u2022 lifecycle bugs: accidental archive of hot data is hard to recover quickly
HOT TIER (high u00b5su20131 ms; expensive)
use: live trading, session state, ad-bidding state
storage: NVMe SSD, in-memory KV (Redis, Memcached)
cost: very high per GB
WARM TIER (low ms)
use: typical OLTP, recent user data, dashboards
storage: SSD-backed DB, S3 Standard, regular Postgres
cost: moderate
COOL TIER (100s of ms; cheap)
use: infrequent access (>30d), reports, last quarter
storage: S3 Standard-IA, Azure Cool Blob, GCS Nearline
cost: ~50% cheaper than hot/warm; retrieval fee
ARCHIVE TIER (seconds-to-hours retrieval; very cheap)
use: compliance archives, backups, year-old logs
storage: S3 Glacier, Azure Archive, GCS Coldline/Archive
cost: ~1/10 of hot; retrieval is slow and metered
OFFLINE / TAPE (hoursu2013days retrieval)
use: compliance (decades), disaster recovery
storage: AWS Snowball + offline tape, on-prem LTO
cost: pennies/TB/month
